compiled_state.title: code-reviewer-deep-arch: reports REVIEW_RESULT: passed when min_score is 3 (should be failed)
compiled_state.author: Main
compiled_state.description:
### Incident Overview
During a deep-tier code review (3 parallel opus reviewers + opus arch synthesis), the arch synthesis agent reported REVIEW_RESULT: passed despite MIN_SCORE=3. The review gate correctly recorded it as failed (score=3), but the sub-agent's output was misleading.

### Observed Behavior at Each Step
1. **Correctness reviewer (opus)**: REVIEW_RESULT: passed, MIN_SCORE=4, 1 minor finding — correct
2. **Verification reviewer (opus)**: REVIEW_RESULT: failed, MIN_SCORE=3, 2 findings (1 important) — correct
3. **Hygiene reviewer (opus)**: REVIEW_RESULT: passed, MIN_SCORE=4, 2 minor findings — correct
4. **Arch synthesis (opus)**: REVIEW_RESULT: passed, MIN_SCORE=3, 3 findings — INCORRECT. Should have reported REVIEW_RESULT: failed because MIN_SCORE=3 does not meet the >=4 threshold.

### Actual Behavior
record-review.sh correctly recorded status=failed (score=3), so the commit gate worked properly. The bug is in the arch agent's self-assessment — it reports 'passed' when it should report 'failed'.

### Expected Behavior
The arch agent should report REVIEW_RESULT: failed when MIN_SCORE < 4, consistent with the review workflow's pass threshold.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. Ticket references sub-agent REVIEW_RESULT behavior AND affected file is agents/code-reviewer-deep-arch.md. Dual-signal detected.
comments.author: Main

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed

comments.body: Bot-psychologist: Missing pass/fail threshold rule in reviewer-base.md. Fix: added explicit 'passed only when every score >= 4' rule.
comments.author: Main
